title: cerastium arvense var. latifolium
intro: Cerastium arvense var. latifolium, also known as broad-leaved field chickweed, is a variation of the field chickweed plant species that is commonly found in meadows and fields. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense var. latifolium include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in salves and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
